trade.summary: Russia-Thailand trade has become a notable bright spot for Russia post-sanctions. Thailand has not sanctioned Russia and continues normal trade. Russia exports energy products, fertilizers, and raw materials. Thailand exports food products, electronics, automotive parts, and rubber goods to Russia. Russian tourists were a major pre-COVID source of revenue for Thailand; Russian tourist numbers have declined but remain significant. Thailand is a popular relocation destination for Russian expatriates post-2022.

diplomatic.status: normal — Thailand has not sanctioned Russia and maintains neutral stance
diplomatic.summary: Thailand has maintained neutral relations with Russia — abstaining or voting against Russia-condemnation resolutions at the UNGA in ways that signal non-alignment. Thailand has not imposed sanctions and continues to accept Russian tourists and maintain diplomatic contacts. The relationship is not particularly strategic but provides Russia with diplomatic breathing space in Southeast Asia. Thailand's military governments have historically had pragmatic relations with non-Western powers.
recent_incidents: Thailand hosted Russian tourists despite Western pressure to restrict
Thailand abstained on some UNGA Ukraine resolutions

military.summary: Limited military relationship. Thailand is a US Major Non-NATO Ally but has shown willingness to purchase from non-Western suppliers. Russia has sold some military equipment to Thailand in the past (Mi-17 helicopters). No current major arms deals. Some defence contacts through multilateral forums. Russia's Ukraine war has complicated defence export reputation.

financial.summary: Thailand has not imposed financial sanctions on Russia. Some Russian capital has sought relocation to Thailand following sanctions. Russian expatriates have brought assets into Thailand. Thai banks have been cautious about Russia-linked transactions due to secondary sanctions risk but have not been formally prohibited. Some Russian investment in Thai real estate.

energy.summary: Thailand imports some Russian oil products through non-direct channels. Thailand has shown interest in accessing discounted Russian energy but must balance this against secondary sanctions risk. Some negotiations on Russian energy supply to Thailand's growing economy.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific collaboration. Some exchange in agricultural science, engineering, and physics. Russian universities have recruited Thai students as part of post-sanctions outreach to non-aligned countries. Rosatom has engaged Thailand on potential nuclear energy cooperation.
